title: Question: Idempotent Payment System
description: Build a small service in Go that simulates a payment system with an idempotent POST /pay endpoint.
expected_behaviors: When a valid payment request is sent for a new transaction ID, it should return a transaction with status 'pending'.
The system should simulate processing and eventually transition the transaction status to 'processed'.
If the exact same transaction ID is sent again, it should return the same transaction (Idempotency), showing the current status (pending or processed).
If the same transaction ID is sent but with a different user ID or amount, it should reject the request to prevent conflicts.
If the amount is <= 0 or the transaction ID is empty, it should return a bad request error.

title: Question: Worker Pool Task Processor
description: Implement a worker pool that processes 100 tasks (squaring numbers) using only 5 concurrent workers, returning results in order.
expected_behaviors: Given 100 tasks and 5 workers, all tasks should be processed concurrently with max 5 workers running at once.
Results should be returned in the original task order (task 1, task 2, ..., task 100).
The execution time should demonstrate concurrency benefits compared to sequential processing.
Each result should show which worker processed it for transparency.

title: Question: Code Review - HTTP Handler
description: Identify problems in the given HTTP handler code and suggest improvements. We've built an interactive playground for you to test both the original (bad) implementation and our fixed (good) implementation.
expected_behaviors: The 'Bad' API should exhibit a race condition under load.
The 'Bad' API will allow infinite payload sizes (DoS vulnerability).
The 'Bad' API will return raw user input (XSS vulnerability).
The 'Good' API correctly restricts payload size, handles errors, and runs concurrently without data corruption.
A complete written analysis should be available outlining all problems and improvements.

title: Question: SQL Left Join & Group By
description: Write a query to list all users and their total order amount, including users with no orders.
expected_behaviors: The result should contain 'name' and 'total_amount'.
Users with no orders should have a total_amount of 0 (or NULL if not coalesced, though 0 is preferred).
The query must execute successfully against the seeded SQLite database.
code:
SELECT 
    u.name, 
    COALESCE(SUM(o.amount), 0) as total_amount
FROM users u
LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
GROUP BY u.id, u.name;

title: Question: SQL Optimization / Indexing
description: Suppose you have a table with millions of rows (`transactions`). Write a query to get the last 10 transactions of a given user (user_id = 123), and suggest one index that would improve query performance.
expected_behaviors: The query should correctly return the last 10 transactions for user_id = 123 ordered by created_at DESC.
The index suggestion should optimize the query (e.g., CREATE INDEX idx_user_created ON transactions(user_id, created_at)).
The Explain Query tool should show 'USING INDEX' instead of 'SCAN TABLE' after the index is applied.
code:
-- Query:
SELECT * FROM transactions 
WHERE user_id = 123 
ORDER BY created_at DESC 
LIMIT 10;

-- Recommended Index:
CREATE INDEX idx_user_created ON transactions(user_id, created_at);
